author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods
book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon II.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is likewise decreed that deacons who have sacrificed and afterwards resumed the conflict, shall enjoy their other honours, but shall abstain from every sacred ministry, neither bringing forth the bread and the cup, nor making proclamations.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Nevertheless, if any of the bishops shall observe in them distress of mind and meek humiliation, it shall be lawful to the bishops to grant more indulgence, or to take away [what has been granted].

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: For Ancient Epitome see above under Canon I.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: In this canon the work and office of a deacon as then understood is set forth, viz.: “to bring forth” (whatever that may mean) “bread or wine” (ἄρτον ἢ ποτηριον ἄναφέρειν) and “to act the herald” (κηρύσσειν).

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: There is considerable difference of opinion as to the meaning of the first of these expressions.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It was always the duty of the deacon to serve the priest, especially when he ministered the Holy Communion, but this phrase may refer to one of two such ministrations, either to bringing the bread and wine to the priest at the offertory, and this is the view of Van Espen, or to the distribution of the Holy Sacrament to the people.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It has been urged that the deacon had ceased to administer the species of bread before the time of this council, but Hefele shews that the custom had not entirely died out.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: If I may be allowed to offer a suggestion, the use of the disjunctive ἢ seems rather to point to the administration of the sacrament than to the bringing of the oblations at the offertory.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: The other diaconal function “to act the herald” refers to the reading of the Holy Gospel, and to the numerous proclamations made by the deacons at mass both according to the Greek and Latin Rite.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: This canon is in the Corpus Juris Canonici united with the foregoing.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Decretum., Pars I., Dist. l., c. xxxii.
